headline_summary: Escalation in Middle East conflict following US-Israeli strikes on Iran.
Gulf states considering military response to Iranian attacks.
Houthi rebels signal resumption of attacks on Red Sea shipping.
Strait of Hormuz sees significant decline in maritime traffic.
Major shipping companies suspend operations in the region.

Energy: Oil prices surge due to supply disruptions.
Increased war risk premiums for shipping insurance.
Potential long-term impacts on global oil supply chains.
Rates: Freight rates expected to rise sharply.
Increased costs for shipping routes around Africa.

risk_drivers: Military escalation in the Gulf region.
Disruption of oil supply through critical chokepoints.

watchlist.why: Increased geopolitical tensions and supply disruptions.
Suspension of operations and increased operational risks.

sources.title: Houthis Signal Renewed Red Sea Shipping Attacks After U.S.–Israeli Strikes on Iran
